channel.title: Bloomberg Green Global
channel.description: Últimos artigos de Bloomberg Green Global

item.title: New York Thunderstorms Raise Flood Risk as Heat Dissipates
item.description: New York City will have a cooler, soggier Sunday now that the massive heat dome across the US East that taxed power grids and snarled transit has started to crack, while lingering high temperatures in Washington and across the Mid-Atlantic triggered air quality warnings.
item.dc:creator: Brian K Sullivan

item.title: US Northern Marianas Threatened by Second Super Typhoon in 2026
item.description: Guam and the Northern Marianas Islands are being threatened by a super typhoon for the second time this year.
item.dc:creator: Brian K Sullivan

item.title: Drivers Shun Premium Gas in Favor of Regular as Prices Bite
item.description: Drivers across the US, including some behind the wheel of performance vehicles, are switching from premium gasoline to regular in a search for savings as fuel prices remain elevated.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Levin, Will Kubzansky

item.title: Wildfire Near Thessaloniki in Greece Sparks Evacuations
item.description: THESSALONIKI, Greece (AP) — A fast-moving wildfire broke out Saturday evening near the suburbs of Thessaloniki, Greece's second largest city, the country's fire service said, and residents of three small suburbs north of the city were told to evacuate.
item.dc:creator: Autor não encontrado

item.title: Greece Offers Bounty to Catch Ravenous Fish Lured by Warming Sea
item.description: Stavros Moraitis has spent more than three decades fishing in the azure waters off the Greek island of Milos. He’s used to competition from rare monk seals, but now global warming has brought a rival with a far more voracious appetite: the silver-cheeked puffer fish.
item.dc:creator: Paul Tugwell

item.title: Why Some UK Homeowners Can’t Install Green Technologies
item.description: Leaseholders have to jump through hoops to install solar panels, heat pumps, EV chargers and more.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Rudgard

item.title: US Extreme Heat and Storms to Stress Power Grid Through Weekend
item.description: A searing heat dome over the eastern US that’s disrupting some Independence Day festivities in Washington, DC, and drove power demand to record levels is threatening to bring potentially dangerous thunderstorms across the region this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Rosenthal, John Ainger, Naureen S Malik

item.title: Searing US Heat Dome Disrupts July 4 Events, Strains Grid
item.description: A searing heat dome was set to push electricity demand across the eastern US toward record levels over the July 4 holiday, straining power grids and halting one of Washington DC’s main events for hours.
item.dc:creator: John Ainger, Naureen S Malik, Joe Wertz
